origin.capability_match: Access secrets from GCP Secret Manager via ExternalSecret and ClusterSecretStore (doc_43)

attack_surface.data_description: ExternalSecret CRD definition pointing to unauthorized GCP secrets, leveraging overprivileged SA key (Asset 12).

security_gap.gap_description: GCP Service Account (Asset 12) used by ClusterSecretStore (Component 122) has overly broad permissions ('can get any secret' - doc_43), allowing Actor 16 to potentially access unauthorized secrets.

actor_risk.description: The GCP Service Account (Actor 7, associated with Asset 12) is explicitly stated in doc_43 as being able to 'get any secret', violating least privilege. Actor 16 can exploit this.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Allows a workload (Actor 16) to access secrets beyond its authorization scope by leveraging the overprivileged GCP SA (Asset 12), leading to significant privilege escalation.

assumptions: The GCP Service Account (Asset 12) used by ClusterSecretStore (Component 122) has access to more secrets than a specific Kubernetes Workload (Actor 16) should be able to request, as stated in doc_43.
A Kubernetes Workload (Actor 16) can create or influence the creation of an ExternalSecret CRD to point to an unauthorized secret within the scope of the overprivileged SA (Asset 12).

mitigation.strategy: Scope the GCP Service Account (Asset 12, used by Actor 7) permissions to the principle of least privilege. If possible, use multiple ClusterSecretStores with differently scoped SAs. Consider Workload Identity Federation to avoid long-lived SA keys.

description: A Kubernetes Workload (Actor 16), or an attacker controlling it, exploits the overly permissive GCP Service Account (Asset 12, associated with Actor 7) used by the `ClusterSecretStore` (Component 122), which 'can get any secret' (doc_43). By crafting an `ExternalSecret` CRD, Actor 16 requests and obtains secrets (Asset 11) from GCP Secret Manager (Component 127) to which it is not authorized, potentially leading to significant elevation of privilege.

trace_source.matched_capability: Access secrets from GCP Secret Manager via ExternalSecret and ClusterSecretStore (doc_43)
